{ "success": true, "message": "Events retrieved successfully", "data": [ { "id": "27", "title": "Precision in Clinical Trials Summit Boston", "slug": "precision-clinical-trials-summit-boston", "description": "\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n

Boston stands at the forefront of the clinical trial industry, renowned for its exceptional concentration of world-class medical institutions, cutting-edge research facilities, and a vibrant biotechnology ecosystem. Boston's robust collaboration between academia, healthcare professionals, and biopharmaceutical companies fosters an environment conducive to innovation and the rapid advancement of medical science. The presence of numerous biotech firms, pharmaceutical companies, and contract research organizations further enhances the city's status as a global leader in clinical research. Boston stands as the epicenter for the world's most prominent cluster of biotech and life science enterprises. What more fitting locale could there be to connect with those spearheading groundbreaking clinical trials?

\r\n

Join us at the 28th Precision in Clinical Trials Summit Boston (PCT) for a two-day journey into the insightful world of clinical trials. You’ll connect with an incredible gathering of experts and uncover practical advice from industry-leading researchers, peers, and executives. Get ready for an event designed to deliver real value-packed insights, connections with fellow professionals, and learn strategies you can use the next day.

\r\n

The Precision in Clinical Trials summit will be a unique platform for the largest biopharmaceutical hub in the region to network and discuss collaboration to achieve their outsourcing and operational strategies.

\r\n

The summit is tailor-made to address the concerns of trial sponsors who are conducting clinical trials at a local and global level. With an innovation-focused agenda, pre-scheduled meetings between potential collaborators, and a host of reliable solution providers, the summit is designed to help attendees find solutions to their most pressing clinical trial challenges in 2026 and beyond. #PCTSummit

\r\n\r\n", "date_from": "2026-04-30", "date_to": "2026-05-01", "venue": "", "location": "", "image": null, "banner_image": null, "status": "1" } ], "count": 1 }

Nathaniel HaferProfessorUMass Chan Medical School
\r\n

Abstract:

\r\n

The COVID-19 pandemic caused unprecedented challenges to the healthcare system. The University of Massachusetts Chan Medical School worked closely and collaboratively with NIH, industry, and the FDA to solicit and co-develop highly meritorious projects developing diagnostics for SARS-CoV-2. Owing to our expertise and track record coordinating large-scale FDA oriented clinical trials for point of care and home-based technologies, we designed innovative study protocols and successfully enrolled over 15,000 racially and geographically diverse individuals from across the nation. The outcome of these studies led to the Emergency Use Authorization of 8 in vitro diagnostics for SARS-CoV-2 and a universal update by the FDA on guidance for use of rapid antigen tests to reduce risk of false negative results. Additionally, our team evaluated a public health program that represented the largest distribution of home-based diagnostics in the country’s history and informed the Biden administration’s widescale distribution of rapid antigen tests. The development, deployment, and analysis of large clinical studies was essential to these outcomes. We will review the key methods and lessons learned from our studies and how this work informs future work for additional indications.

Speaker:

Roya Khosravi-FarPresident and CEOInnoTech Precision Medicine, Inc.

Abstract:

\r\n

Early diagnosis in oncology can drastically improve patient outcomes by enabling more
timely and effective treatments while reducing the overall burden on healthcare systems.
Detecting cancer at earlier stages increases the chances of successful intervention,
allowing for less aggressive therapies and better survival rates. It also plays a pivotal role in
clinical trials, where early identification of molecular markers helps with precise patient
stratification, timely therapeutic intervention, and the ability to monitor treatment
responses in real time. Despite these benefits, challenges remain in developing sensitive,
specific, and non-invasive tools for early cancer detection, which is critical for advancing
both clinical care and research.
InnoTech Precision Medicine's platform technology is designed to address these
challenges by offering a non-invasive, multiplexed diagnostic system capable of detecting
both genotypic and phenotypic biomarkers at the point of care. This versatile technology
can be applied to various cancer types, enabling early diagnosis that leads to more timely
and effective treatment. A key example is its use in oropharyngeal squamous cell
carcinoma (OPSCC), where the platform detects genotypic and phenotypic biomarkers
simultaneously in saliva, allowing for the early identification and non-invasive detection of
of HPV-driven cancers. By diagnosing OPSCC at an earlier stage, treatment can begin
sooner, increasing the likelihood of success and reducing the need for more invasive
interventions.
Moreover, InnoTech’s platform has significant implications for oncology clinical trials.

\r\n

Its real-time molecular marker analysis can enhance patient selection, allowing for better
stratification based on likely treatment responses, and help monitor therapy effectiveness
as trials progress. By integrating early detection with ongoing molecular insights, InnoTech’s
technology not only improves clinical care but also accelerates the development of new
cancer therapies, making it an invaluable tool for the future of oncology research.
